subject: English
chapter: The Book That Saved the Earth
author: Claire Boiko

activities_projects.title: List of Characters
activities_projects.objective: Identify the characters in the play
steps: Read the play carefully
List all the characters mentioned
Write one line about each character

activities_projects.title: Setting of the Play
activities_projects.objective: Understand the time and place of the play
steps: Read the introduction of the play
Identify the time period
Identify the main places shown in the play

activities_projects.title: Think-Tank Character Traits
steps: Read Think-Tank’s dialogues
Write 3–4 points describing his nature

activities_projects.title: Role of Noodle
activities_projects.objective: Understand Noodle’s role in the play
steps: Read the scenes involving Noodle
Write three sentences explaining how he helps Think-Tank

activities_projects.title: Why Earth Was Not Invaded
activities_projects.objective: Understand the central idea of the play
steps: Think about the Martians’ reactions
Write 3–4 sentences explaining why Earth was saved

activities_projects.title: Importance of Books
activities_projects.objective: Understand the message of the play
steps: Think about how books helped save Earth
Write 3–4 sentences on the importance of books

activities_projects.title: Funny Moments in the Play
steps: Read the scenes carefully
List two funny situations from the play

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Builder
steps: Pick five new words from the play
Write their meanings
Use each word in a sentence

activities_projects.title: Moral of the Play
steps: Think about the lesson conveyed
Write the moral in your own words

activities_projects.title: Real-Life Connection with Reading
activities_projects.objective: Relate the play to real life
steps: Think about the role of books in your life
Write 3–4 sentences connecting it to the play
